L1.area: Windows I/O event notification and completion port delivery layer

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the event notification mechanism for Windows I/O resources. When an I/O resource is deregistered from the event registry, previously queued completion port events may still be delivered with stale tokens that reference already-freed memory. Applications that store pointers in event tokens may experience invalid memory access when processing these stale events.

L3.area: Windows named pipe I/O completion port event notification and readiness delivery
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the event notification mechanism for Windows named pipes. When a named pipe is deregistered from the event registry, completion port events may still be posted and delivered with stale tokens that reference already-freed memory. Applications that store pointers in event tokens, such as async runtimes, may experience use-after-free when processing these stale events after deregistration.

ground_truth.reason: The named pipe readiness notification functions post IOCP completion events without checking whether the pipe has been deregistered, allowing stale tokens referencing freed memory to be delivered to consumers.
